core_thesis: The author describes seven self-hosted applications that are critical to his homelab infrastructure and run continuously, emphasizing reliability, ease of use, and integration with his smart home and media consumption. The article serves as a practical guide for homelab enthusiasts looking to build a stable, always-on server environment.
detailed_summary: The article lists seven self-hosted apps that the author runs 24/7 in his homelab: Plex Media Server for media streaming, Scrypted for local NVR and HomeKit camera integration, Home Assistant for smart home automation, Pterodactyl for game server management, Audiobookshelf for self-hosted audiobooks, Speedtest-Tracker for internet speed monitoring, and Nginx Proxy Manager for reverse proxy and SSL management. Each app is described with its purpose, why it's always on, and how it integrates into the author's daily life. The author also mentions Portainer as an honorable mention for container management. The article emphasizes the importance of reliability and minimal downtime for these services.

entities.name: Plex Media Server
entities.role: Media streaming server for movies, TV shows, and personal media.

entities.name: Scrypted
entities.role: Self-hosted NVR and camera integration with HomeKit.

entities.name: Home Assistant
entities.role: Smart home automation platform controlling non-HomeKit devices.

entities.name: Pterodactyl
entities.role: Game server management panel for Minecraft, Satisfactory, Ark, etc.

entities.name: Audiobookshelf
entities.role: Self-hosted audiobook server with streaming and offline support.

entities.name: Speedtest-Tracker
entities.role: Internet speed monitoring tool with historical data.

entities.name: Nginx Proxy Manager
entities.role: Reverse proxy and SSL certificate manager for web services.

key_data_points: Plex is run 24/7 with updates scheduled only once per week early Sunday morning to minimize downtime (🟢 relevant to homelab: ensures media availability at all times).
Scrypted unifies all cameras to record locally and integrates with HomeKit, eliminating cloud dependency (🟢 relevant to homelab: improves privacy and reliability).
Home Assistant controls smart home scenes via non-HomeKit devices, running 24/7/365 (🟢 relevant to homelab: critical for automation reliability).
Pterodactyl manages multiple game servers (Minecraft, Satisfactory, Ark) with minimal downtime, only for maintenance (🟢 relevant to homelab: useful for hosting game servers for friends).
Audiobookshelf replaces Audible, allowing streaming to save iPhone storage; server must always be available (🟢 relevant to homelab: demonstrates self-hosting for media).
Speedtest-Tracker runs in background to log internet speed history for troubleshooting (🟢 relevant to homelab: useful for ISP performance monitoring).
Nginx Proxy Manager routes traffic to subdomains for all web services; if it goes down, most services become inaccessible (🟢 relevant to homelab: essential for external access).
Portainer is an honorable mention for easy Docker container management via GUI (🟢 relevant to homelab: simplifies container administration).
conclusions: The author prioritizes reliability and minimal downtime for core homelab services, scheduling updates during low-usage periods. This approach ensures that media, smart home, game servers, and external web services are always accessible.
Self-hosting these apps reduces reliance on cloud services (e.g., Scrypted for cameras, Audiobookshelf for audiobooks) and provides greater control over data and availability.
Nginx Proxy Manager is a critical component for exposing multiple services securely via reverse proxy, making it a must-have for any homelab with external access needs.
personal_relevance_summary: This article is highly relevant to your homelab and DevOps interests, providing a concrete list of always-on services that enhance reliability and functionality. You can adopt similar practices for media streaming (Plex), smart home automation (Home Assistant), game server hosting (Pterodactyl), and secure external access (Nginx Proxy Manager). The emphasis on minimal downtime and scheduled updates is a practical tip for maintaining a stable homelab. Additionally, the mention of Portainer as a management tool aligns with your programming background, offering a GUI to simplify Docker container management.
extra_advice: Consider integrating these services with a monitoring tool like Uptime Kuma or Grafana to track uptime and performance. For game servers, you could automate backups using Pterodactyl's built-in features or a cron job. Also, explore using Tailscale or Cloudflare Tunnel with Nginx Proxy Manager for secure remote access without exposing ports.
